packageRules.description: Python interpreter bumps (requires-python) and the matching python-base container image ship together in one PR, separate from regular 3pp bumps. Manual review: bumping the interpreter is rarely a no-op for our 3pp set, so we don't want this automerged alongside boto3 patches.
Pair the python-base Docker image (builder + runner, any registry path) with the interpreter bump above via the shared groupSlug.

Update only the lockfile to the latest in-range version, leaving the declared range in pyproject.toml untouched. Example: `cachetools >=5,<8` locked at 7.1.2 becomes uv.lock at 7.1.4 while pyproject.toml still reads `>=5,<8`. This keeps the declared range wide so downstream consumers resolving from our published wheels can pick compatible versions, while we test and pin the latest in-range version via the lockfile. Renovate runs the manager's lock tool (`uv lock`, `poetry lock`, etc.) to refresh the lockfile. Scoped to lockfile-aware managers only: pip_requirements and pip_setup are intentionally excluded because they typically have no separate lockfile for update-lockfile to update.
